# UC-17.1.57 · ISE ERS / OpenAPI Brute-Force, Token Theft, and Anomalous API-Caller Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Audit &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*ISE has an interface for other systems to connect to it. We watch for too many wrong passwords or unusual patterns of use — these can mean someone is trying to break in or has stolen a password.*

---

## Description

Detects ERS / OpenAPI brute-force attempts, token theft (one user from many IPs), and anomalous high-volume callers — symptoms of compromised API credentials or misuse.

## Value

ISE's ERS/OpenAPI exposes the entire control plane. A compromised API token can be used to read endpoint inventory, modify auth policies, or push ANC actions. Detecting brute-force or anomalous use within 5 minutes is the primary defense.

## Implementation

Confirm *Administrative and Operational Audit* at INFO. Schedule every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, throttle on `api_user` for 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Run a script that attempts 51 wrong-password ERS calls. UC-17.1.57 fires for that user with `count=51`.

### Negative scenario

Successful API calls. UC-17.1.57 does not fire.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- ISE ERS / OpenAPI enabled.
- `api_users.csv` with `api_user`, `expected_caller_ip`, `expected_endpoint_paths`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Administrative and Operational Audit* at INFO.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field.

A secondary anomaly check matches `client_ip` against `api_users.csv` `expected_caller_ip`:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=52001 earliest=-1h
| lookup api_users.csv api_user OUTPUT expected_caller_ip
| where !match(client_ip, expected_caller_ip)
```

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — API Access Audit":
- Single value: failures last 1h.
- Top-N users.
- Map of caller IPs.

Alerting: PagerDuty high on brute-force; medium on caller-IP anomaly.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **Bursts of 52004 (rate-limit)** — Tune ISE rate-limit OR fix the offending integration's polling cadence.
- **52002 from internal dev IP** — Dev iterating on a script; coordinate.
- **Persistent 52003 (authz denied)** — User has wrong role; review.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (52002, 52003, 52004)
| stats count, dc(client_ip) as unique_ips, dc(endpoint_path) as unique_paths by api_user
| where count > 50 OR (unique_ips > 5 AND count > 20)
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: API-failure events last 1h. (2) Top-N: API users by failure count. (3) Map: client_ip distribution.

## Known False Positives

- **Splunk-side polling burst** — Multiple Splunk modular inputs polling ISE simultaneously may briefly trigger; tag those service accounts.
- **CMDB sync bursts** — Periodic CMDB sync produces high-volume reads.
- **First-day deployment of integration** — Integration testing produces failure bursts.

## References

- [Cisco ISE OpenAPI Documentation](https://developer.cisco.com/docs/ise/3.4/openapi/)
